title: helianthemum pilosum var. asperum
intro: Helianthemum pilosum var. asperum, also known as the rough rockrose, is a variety of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae, commonly found in the Mediterranean region. The rough rockrose plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, soothing wounds, and protecting against infections. The rough rockrose plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, although its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal herb require further study and validation.
